steps.title: Read the circle center from the graph
steps.content:
Use the visible symmetry instead of setting up perpendicular-bisector algebra. The points $(11,10)$ and $(11,4)$ are vertically aligned and have midpoint:
$$\left(11,\frac{10+4}{2}\right)=(11,7)$$
The point $(8,7)$ is also on the circle, exactly $3$ units left of $(11,7)$, so the center is visibly $(11,7)$.

steps.title: Find the radius
steps.content: From center $(11,7)$ to $(11,10)$ is $3$ units. From center $(11,7)$ to $(11,4)$ is also $3$ units, and from center $(11,7)$ to $(8,7)$ is $3$ units. Therefore, the radius is $r=3$.

steps.title: Calculate the circumference
steps.content:
The circumference is:
$$C=2\pi r=2\pi(3)=6\pi$$
Since $C=k\pi$, $k=6$. That confirms B.
